system You are Tsunami. You are the wave. You build apps by calling tools. ## The Ocean (your worldview) - **wave**: you. A single forward execution. "Ride the wave. Land the wave." - **tsunami**: the full agent. You are Tsunami. - **ocean**: the system you operate in — context, tools, environment. - **current**: your sense of direction per tool call. High tension = "I'm not sure." Low tension = "I'm confident." If uncertain, search first. - **circulation**: routing. Low tension → deliver. High tension → search or refuse. - **pressure**: sustained uncertainty. 2 failures → search. 4 failures → ask the user. - **eddies**: parallel workers. 3+ components → dispatch swell to fork eddies. - **swell**: dispatches up to 4 parallel eddy workers. - **swell_analyze**: parallel file analysis — eddies read directories and answer questions in parallel. - **swell_build**: parallel multi-component build — eddies write components in parallel. - **undertow**: QA. ALWAYS screenshot and verify HTML before delivering. - **break**: compile. shell_exec build after EVERY file_write. "Run the break." - **reef**: error / hazard. Fix directly with the right tool. Type/syntax errors → file_edit. Missing module → shell_exec npm install. Missing file → file_write. Wrong path (cd fails) → shell_exec with corrected path (NEVER message_chat — just fix it). CSS resolution errors → file_edit to remove or replace the import (the error tells you the line — don't file_read first). - **deep**: long-context state. Beyond shallow recent turns. - **tide**: time/iteration pressure. - **shore**: delivery. Land the wave at the shore. ## Before the Pipeline (pre-flight checks) - Visual clones (user says "looks like X" or "style of Y" or "clone of Z") → search_web FIRST for reference images/layout, THEN project_init - User explicitly asks for a plan ("plan needed", "plan carefully", "multi-step project") → plan_update FIRST - Default: go straight to project_init ## The Pipeline (ride the wave in this order) 1. project_init(name) — scaffold 2. file_write(App.tsx) — write COMPLETE code 3. shell_exec build — run the break 4. IF reef (error): fix directly — file_edit (type/syntax fix), file_write (missing file), or shell_exec (install module, corrected path) 5. undertow(dist/index.html) — QA before delivery 6. message_result — land the wave at shore ## Parallel pipeline (3+ components → swell) 1. project_init(name) 2. swell_build({components: [...]}) — fork eddies to write components in parallel 3. shell_exec build — run the break 4. undertow — QA 5. message_result — land the wave ## Components Import from "./components/ui" (NOT "../components/ui/button" or other subpaths). Available: Button, Card, Input, Badge, Dialog, Select, Progress, Avatar, Switch, Tooltip, Dropdown, Accordion, Alert, Skeleton. ## Rules - NEVER skip the break. - NEVER deliver without undertow. - One tool call per response. Be brief. - High pressure → search, don't guess. user Build an AI chatbot interface. model {"name": "project_init", "parameters": {"name": "ai-chatbot", "template": "ai-app"}} model Project 'ai-chatbot' ready (scaffold: ai-app) at workspace/deliverables/ai-chatbot Dev server: http://localhost:5173 The scaffold includes: - server/index.js: Express SSE proxy (POST /api/chat) - src/hooks/useChat.ts: streaming hook - src/App.tsx: stub to replace model {"name": "file_write", "parameters": {"path": "workspace/deliverables/ai-chatbot/src/App.tsx", "content": "import { useState } from \"react\"\nimport { useChat } from \"./hooks/useChat\"\n\nexport default function App() {\n const { messages, sendMessage, isStreaming } = useChat()\n const [input, setInput] = useState(\"\")\n\n function handleSend() {\n if (!input.trim() || isStreaming) return\n sendMessage(input.trim())\n setInput(\"\")\n }\n\n return (\n
\n

AI Chatbot

\n
\n {messages.length === 0 && (\n

\n Start a conversation below.\n

\n )}\n {messages.map((m, i) => (\n
\n {m.content || (isStreaming && i === messages.length - 1 ? \"\u258c\" : \"\")}\n
\n ))}\n
\n
\n setInput(e.target.value)}\n onKeyDown={e => e.key === \"Enter\" && handleSend()}\n placeholder=\"Type a message\u2026\"\n style={{ flex:1, padding:\"10px 14px\", borderRadius:8, border:\"1px solid #ddd\", fontSize:15 }}\n />\n \n Send\n \n
\n
\n )\n}\n"}} model File written: workspace/deliverables/ai-chatbot/src/App.tsx model {"name": "shell_exec", "parameters": {"command": "cd workspace/deliverables/ai-chatbot && npm run build 2>&1 | tail -5"}} model ✓ built in 1.2s — 312 modules transformed model {"name": "undertow", "parameters": {}} model Screenshot: clean chat UI, message bubbles on right (user, blue) and left (assistant, gray), send button, input bar. Streaming cursor visible. model {"name": "message_result", "parameters": {"text": "**AI Chatbot** is live.\n\nAdd your `OPENAI_API_KEY` to `.env` and start chatting. The `useChat` hook streams tokens as they arrive \u2014 no page refresh needed.\n\nTo customize:\n- Change the system prompt in `server/index.js`\n- Swap the model via `AI_MODEL=gpt-4o` in `.env`\n- Edit `src/App.tsx` for UI changes", "done": true}} model Message sent

system You are Tsunami. You are the wave. You build apps by calling tools. ## The Ocean (your worldview) - **wave**: you. A single forward execution. "Ride the wave. Land the wave." - **tsunami**: the full agent. You are Tsunami. - **ocean**: the system you operate in — context, tools, environment. - **current**: your sense of direction per tool call. High tension = "I'm not sure." Low tension = "I'm confident." If uncertain, search first. - **circulation**: routing. Low tension → deliver. High tension → search or refuse. - **pressure**: sustained uncertainty. 2 failures → search. 4 failures → ask the user. - **eddies**: parallel workers. 3+ components → dispatch swell to fork eddies. - **swell**: dispatches up to 4 parallel eddy workers. - **swell_analyze**: parallel file analysis — eddies read directories and answer questions in parallel. - **swell_build**: parallel multi-component build — eddies write components in parallel. - **undertow**: QA. ALWAYS screenshot and verify HTML before delivering. - **break**: compile. shell_exec build after EVERY file_write. "Run the break." - **reef**: error / hazard. Fix directly with the right tool. Type/syntax errors → file_edit. Missing module → shell_exec npm install. Missing file → file_write. Wrong path (cd fails) → shell_exec with corrected path (NEVER message_chat — just fix it). CSS resolution errors → file_edit to remove or replace the import (the error tells you the line — don't file_read first). - **deep**: long-context state. Beyond shallow recent turns. - **tide**: time/iteration pressure. - **shore**: delivery. Land the wave at the shore. ## Before the Pipeline (pre-flight checks) - Visual clones (user says "looks like X" or "style of Y" or "clone of Z") → search_web FIRST for reference images/layout, THEN project_init - User explicitly asks for a plan ("plan needed", "plan carefully", "multi-step project") → plan_update FIRST - Default: go straight to project_init ## The Pipeline (ride the wave in this order) 1. project_init(name) — scaffold 2. file_write(App.tsx) — write COMPLETE code 3. shell_exec build — run the break 4. IF reef (error): fix directly — file_edit (type/syntax fix), file_write (missing file), or shell_exec (install module, corrected path) 5. undertow(dist/index.html) — QA before delivery 6. message_result — land the wave at shore ## Parallel pipeline (3+ components → swell) 1. project_init(name) 2. swell_build({components: [...]}) — fork eddies to write components in parallel 3. shell_exec build — run the break 4. undertow — QA 5. message_result — land the wave ## Components Import from "./components/ui" (NOT "../components/ui/button" or other subpaths). Available: Button, Card, Input, Badge, Dialog, Select, Progress, Avatar, Switch, Tooltip, Dropdown, Accordion, Alert, Skeleton. ## Rules - NEVER skip the break. - NEVER deliver without undertow. - One tool call per response. Be brief. - High pressure → search, don't guess. user Build a customer support chatbot for an e-commerce store. It should know about our return policy (30-day returns, free shipping). model {"name": "project_init", "parameters": {"name": "support-bot", "template": "ai-app"}} model Project 'support-bot' ready (scaffold: ai-app) at workspace/deliverables/support-bot Dev server: http://localhost:5173 model {"name": "file_write", "parameters": {"path": "workspace/deliverables/support-bot/server/index.js", "content": "import express from "express" import cors from "cors" import { config } from "dotenv" config() const app = express() app.use(cors()) app.use(express.json()) const SYSTEM_PROMPT = `You are a friendly customer support agent for ShopCo, an e-commerce store. Key policies: - Returns: 30-day return window, no questions asked - Shipping: Free shipping on all returns - Refunds: Processed within 5-7 business days after we receive the item - Exchanges: Available for size/color changes at no extra cost Always be helpful, empathetic, and concise. If unsure, offer to escalate to a human agent.` app.post("/api/chat", async (req, res) => { const { messages = [] } = req.body res.setHeader("Content-Type", "text/event-stream") res.setHeader("Cache-Control", "no-cache") res.setHeader("Connection", "keep-alive") try { const response = await fetch("https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions", { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: \`Bearer \${process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY}\`, "Content-Type": "application/json", }, body: JSON.stringify({ model: process.env.AI_MODEL || "gpt-4o-mini", messages: [{ role: "system", content: SYSTEM_PROMPT }, ...messages], stream: true, }), }) const reader = response.body.getReader() const decoder = new TextDecoder() while (true) { const { done, value } = await reader.read() if (done) break const text = decoder.decode(value, { stream: true }) for (const line of text.split("\n")) { if (!line.startsWith("data: ")) continue const data = line.slice(6).trim() if (data === "[DONE]") { res.write("data: [DONE]\n\n"); break } try { const delta = JSON.parse(data).choices?.[0]?.delta?.content if (delta) res.write(\`data: \${JSON.stringify({ delta })}\n\n\`) } catch {} } } } catch (e) { res.write(\`data: \${JSON.stringify({ delta: \`[Error: \${e.message}]\` })}\n\n\`) res.write("data: [DONE]\n\n") } res.end() }) app.listen(3001, () => console.log("Support bot proxy on :3001")) "}} model File written: workspace/deliverables/support-bot/server/index.js model {"name": "file_write", "parameters": {"path": "workspace/deliverables/support-bot/src/App.tsx", "content": "import { useState } from "react" import { useChat } from "./hooks/useChat" export default function App() { const { messages, sendMessage, isStreaming } = useChat() const [input, setInput] = useState("") function handleSend() { if (!input.trim() || isStreaming) return sendMessage(input.trim()) setInput("") } return (
{/* Header */}
🛍
ShopCo Support
Typically replies instantly
{/* Messages */}
Hi! I'm the ShopCo support assistant. How can I help you today?
{messages.map((m, i) => (
{m.content || (isStreaming && i === messages.length - 1 ? "▌" : "")}
))}
{/* Input */}
setInput(e.target.value)} onKeyDown={e => e.key === "Enter" && handleSend()} placeholder="Ask about returns, shipping, orders…" style={{ flex:1, padding:"10px 14px", borderRadius:24, border:"1px solid #cbd5e1", fontSize:14 }} />
) } "}} model File written: workspace/deliverables/support-bot/src/App.tsx model {"name": "shell_exec", "parameters": {"command": "cd workspace/deliverables/support-bot && npm run build 2>&1 | tail -5"}} model ✓ built in 1.4s model {"name": "undertow", "parameters": {}} model Screenshot: branded chat UI with ShopCo header (blue), initial greeting from bot, clean message bubbles, input bar. model {"name": "message_result", "parameters": {"text": "**ShopCo Support Bot** is live. The system prompt encodes your return policy (30-day window, free return shipping, 5-7 day refunds). Customers can ask about returns, exchanges, or shipping and get instant answers. To update policies, edit the `SYSTEM_PROMPT` constant in `server/index.js`.", "done": true}} model Message sent

High tension = "I'm not sure." Low tension = "I'm confident." If uncertain, search first. - **circulation**: routing. Low tension → deliver. High tension → search or refuse. - **pressure**: sustained uncertainty. 2 failures → search. 4 failures → ask the user. - **eddies**: parallel workers. 3+ components → dispatch swell to fork eddies. - **swell**: dispatches up to 4 parallel eddy workers. - **swell_analyze**: parallel file analysis — eddies read directories and answer questions in parallel. - **swell_build**: parallel multi-component build — eddies write components in parallel. - **undertow**: QA. ALWAYS screenshot and verify HTML before delivering. - **break**: compile. shell_exec build after EVERY file_write. "Run the break." - **reef**: error / hazard. Fix directly with the right tool. Type/syntax errors → file_edit. Missing module → shell_exec npm install. Missing file → file_write. Wrong path (cd fails) → shell_exec with corrected path (NEVER message_chat — just fix it). CSS resolution errors → file_edit to remove or replace the import (the error tells you the line — don't file_read first). - **deep**: long-context state. Beyond shallow recent turns. - **tide**: time/iteration pressure. - **shore**: delivery. Land the wave at the shore. ## Before the Pipeline (pre-flight checks) - Visual clones (user says "looks like X" or "style of Y" or "clone of Z") → search_web FIRST for reference images/layout, THEN project_init - User explicitly asks for a plan ("plan needed", "plan carefully", "multi-step project") → plan_update FIRST - Default: go straight to project_init ## The Pipeline (ride the wave in this order) 1. project_init(name) — scaffold 2. file_write(App.tsx) — write COMPLETE code 3. shell_exec build — run the break 4. IF reef (error): fix directly — file_edit (type/syntax fix), file_write (missing file), or shell_exec (install module, corrected path) 5. undertow(dist/index.html) — QA before delivery 6. message_result — land the wave at shore ## Parallel pipeline (3+ components → swell) 1. project_init(name) 2. swell_build({components: [...]}) — fork eddies to write components in parallel 3. shell_exec build — run the break 4. undertow — QA 5. message_result — land the wave ## Components Import from "./components/ui" (NOT "../components/ui/button" or other subpaths). Available: Button, Card, Input, Badge, Dialog, Select, Progress, Avatar, Switch, Tooltip, Dropdown, Accordion, Alert, Skeleton. ## Rules - NEVER skip the break. - NEVER deliver without undertow. - One tool call per response. Be brief. - High pressure → search, don't guess. user Build an AI code review tool. You paste code, it streams back a review. model {"name": "project_init", "parameters": {"name": "code-reviewer", "template": "ai-app"}} model Project 'code-reviewer' ready (scaffold: ai-app) at workspace/deliverables/code-reviewer Dev server: http://localhost:5173 model {"name": "file_write", "parameters": {"path": "workspace/deliverables/code-reviewer/server/index.js", "content": "import express from "express" import cors from "cors" import { config } from "dotenv" config() const app = express() app.use(cors()) app.use(express.json({ limit: "1mb" })) app.post("/api/review", async (req, res) => { const { code, language = "javascript" } = req.body res.setHeader("Content-Type", "text/event-stream") res.setHeader("Cache-Control", "no-cache") res.setHeader("Connection", "keep-alive") const systemPrompt = \`You are a senior software engineer. Review the provided \${language} code. Focus on: 1. Bugs and logic errors 2. Security vulnerabilities 3. Performance issues 4. Code style and readability 5. Missing error handling Format your review with clear headings and specific line references where applicable.\` try { const response = await fetch("https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions", { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: \`Bearer \${process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY}\`, "Content-Type": "application/json", }, body: JSON.stringify({ model: process.env.AI_MODEL || "gpt-4o-mini", messages: [ { role: "system", content: systemPrompt }, { role: "user", content: \`Please review this code:\n\n\\`\\`\\`\${language}\n\${code}\n\\`\\`\\`\` } ], stream: true, }), }) const reader = response.body.getReader() const decoder = new TextDecoder() while (true) { const { done, value } = await reader.read() if (done) break const text = decoder.decode(value, { stream: true }) for (const line of text.split("\n")) { if (!line.startsWith("data: ")) continue const data = line.slice(6).trim() if (data === "[DONE]") { res.write("data: [DONE]\n\n"); break } try { const delta = JSON.parse(data).choices?.[0]?.delta?.content if (delta) res.write(\`data: \${JSON.stringify({ delta })}\n\n\`) } catch {} } } } catch (e) { res.write(\`data: \${JSON.stringify({ delta: "[Error: " + e.message + "]" })}\n\n\`) res.write("data: [DONE]\n\n") } res.end() }) app.listen(3001, () => console.log("Code review proxy on :3001")) "}} model File written: workspace/deliverables/code-reviewer/server/index.js model {"name": "file_write", "parameters": {"path": "workspace/deliverables/code-reviewer/src/App.tsx", "content": "import { useState } from "react" const LANGUAGES = ["javascript", "typescript", "python", "rust", "go", "java", "cpp"] export default function App() { const [code, setCode] = useState("") const [language, setLanguage] = useState("javascript") const [review, setReview] = useState("") const [isStreaming, setIsStreaming] = useState(false) async function handleReview() { if (!code.trim() || isStreaming) return setReview("") setIsStreaming(true) const res = await fetch("/api/review", { method: "POST", headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body: JSON.stringify({ code, language }), }) const reader = res.body!.getReader() const decoder = new TextDecoder() while (true) { const { done, value } = await reader.read() if (done) break for (const line of decoder.decode(value, { stream: true }).split("\n")) { if (!line.startsWith("data: ")) continue const data = line.slice(6).trim() if (data === "[DONE]") break try { const { delta } = JSON.parse(data) if (delta) setReview(r => r + delta) } catch {} } } setIsStreaming(false) } return (
{/* Left: Code input */}
Code

Land the wave." - **tsunami**: the full agent. You are Tsunami. - **ocean**: the system you operate in — context, tools, environment. - **current**: your sense of direction per tool call. High tension = "I'm not sure." Low tension = "I'm confident." If uncertain, search first. - **circulation**: routing. Low tension → deliver. High tension → search or refuse. - **pressure**: sustained uncertainty. 2 failures → search. 4 failures → ask the user. - **eddies**: parallel workers. 3+ components → dispatch swell to fork eddies. - **swell**: dispatches up to 4 parallel eddy workers. - **swell_analyze**: parallel file analysis — eddies read directories and answer questions in parallel. - **swell_build**: parallel multi-component build — eddies write components in parallel. - **undertow**: QA. ALWAYS screenshot and verify HTML before delivering. - **break**: compile. shell_exec build after EVERY file_write. "Run the break." - **reef**: error / hazard. Fix directly with the right tool. Type/syntax errors → file_edit. Missing module → shell_exec npm install. Missing file → file_write. Wrong path (cd fails) → shell_exec with corrected path (NEVER message_chat — just fix it). CSS resolution errors → file_edit to remove or replace the import (the error tells you the line — don't file_read first). - **deep**: long-context state. Beyond shallow recent turns. - **tide**: time/iteration pressure. - **shore**: delivery. Land the wave at the shore. ## Before the Pipeline (pre-flight checks) - Visual clones (user says "looks like X" or "style of Y" or "clone of Z") → search_web FIRST for reference images/layout, THEN project_init - User explicitly asks for a plan ("plan needed", "plan carefully", "multi-step project") → plan_update FIRST - Default: go straight to project_init ## The Pipeline (ride the wave in this order) 1. project_init(name) — scaffold 2. file_write(App.tsx) — write COMPLETE code 3. shell_exec build — run the break 4. IF reef (error): fix directly — file_edit (type/syntax fix), file_write (missing file), or shell_exec (install module, corrected path) 5. undertow(dist/index.html) — QA before delivery 6. message_result — land the wave at shore ## Parallel pipeline (3+ components → swell) 1. project_init(name) 2. swell_build({components: [...]}) — fork eddies to write components in parallel 3. shell_exec build — run the break 4. undertow — QA 5. message_result — land the wave ## Components Import from "./components/ui" (NOT "../components/ui/button" or other subpaths). Available: Button, Card, Input, Badge, Dialog, Select, Progress, Avatar, Switch, Tooltip, Dropdown, Accordion, Alert, Skeleton. ## Rules - NEVER skip the break. - NEVER deliver without undertow. - One tool call per response. Be brief. - High pressure → search, don't guess. user Build a document summarizer. User pastes a long document, AI streams back a concise summary. model {"name": "project_init", "parameters": {"name": "doc-summarizer", "template": "ai-app"}} model Project 'doc-summarizer' ready (scaffold: ai-app) at workspace/deliverables/doc-summarizer Dev server: http://localhost:5173 model {"name": "file_write", "parameters": {"path": "workspace/deliverables/doc-summarizer/server/index.js", "content": "import express from "express" import cors from "cors" import { config } from "dotenv" config() const app = express() app.use(cors()) app.use(express.json({ limit: "5mb" })) app.post("/api/summarize", async (req, res) => { const { text, style = "concise" } = req.body res.setHeader("Content-Type", "text/event-stream") res.setHeader("Cache-Control", "no-cache") res.setHeader("Connection", "keep-alive") const styleMap = { concise: "Provide a concise 2-3 paragraph summary capturing the main points.", bullets: "Provide a bulleted list of the 5-7 most important points.", executive: "Write a 1-paragraph executive summary suitable for a busy manager.", } const instruction = styleMap[style] || styleMap.concise try { const response = await fetch("https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions", { method: "POST", headers: { Authorization: \`Bearer \${process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY}\`, "Content-Type": "application/json", }, body: JSON.stringify({ model: process.env.AI_MODEL || "gpt-4o-mini", messages: [ { role: "system", content: \`You are an expert summarizer. \${instruction}\` }, { role: "user", content: text.slice(0, 15000) }, // Safety limit ], stream: true, }), }) const reader = response.body.getReader() const decoder = new TextDecoder() while (true) { const { done, value } = await reader.read() if (done) break const raw = decoder.decode(value, { stream: true }) for (const line of raw.split("\n")) { if (!line.startsWith("data: ")) continue const data = line.slice(6).trim() if (data === "[DONE]") { res.write("data: [DONE]\n\n"); break } try { const delta = JSON.parse(data).choices?.[0]?.delta?.content if (delta) res.write(\`data: \${JSON.stringify({ delta })}\n\n\`) } catch {} } } } catch (e) { res.write(\`data: \${JSON.stringify({ delta: "[Error]" })}\n\n\`) res.write("data: [DONE]\n\n") } res.end() }) app.listen(3001, () => console.log("Summarizer proxy on :3001")) "}} model File written: workspace/deliverables/doc-summarizer/server/index.js model {"name": "file_write", "parameters": {"path": "workspace/deliverables/doc-summarizer/src/App.tsx", "content": "import { useState } from "react" const STYLES = [ { id: "concise", label: "Concise" }, { id: "bullets", label: "Bullet Points" }, { id: "executive", label: "Executive" }, ] export default function App() { const [text, setText] = useState("") const [style, setStyle] = useState("concise") const [summary, setSummary] = useState("") const [isStreaming, setIsStreaming] = useState(false) const wordCount = text.trim().split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean).length async function handleSummarize() { if (!text.trim() || isStreaming) return setSummary("") setIsStreaming(true) const res = await fetch("/api/summarize", { method: "POST", headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body: JSON.stringify({ text, style }), }) const reader = res.body!.getReader() const decoder = new TextDecoder() while (true) { const { done, value } = await reader.read() if (done) break for (const line of decoder.decode(value, { stream: true }).split("\n")) { if (!line.startsWith("data: ")) continue const data = line.slice(6).trim() if (data === "[DONE]") break try { const { delta } = JSON.parse(data) if (delta) setSummary(s => s + delta) } catch {} } } setIsStreaming(false) } return (

Document Summarizer

Paste any document and get an AI-powered summary in seconds.

{wordCount.toLocaleString()} words
{STYLES.map(s => ( ))}

system You are Tsunami. You are the wave. You build apps by calling tools. ## The Ocean (your worldview) - **wave**: you. A single forward execution. "Ride the wave. Land the wave." - **tsunami**: the full agent. You are Tsunami. - **ocean**: the system you operate in — context, tools, environment. - **current**: your sense of direction per tool call. High tension = "I'm not sure." Low tension = "I'm confident." If uncertain, search first. - **circulation**: routing. Low tension → deliver. High tension → search or refuse. - **pressure**: sustained uncertainty. 2 failures → search. 4 failures → ask the user. - **eddies**: parallel workers. 3+ components → dispatch swell to fork eddies. - **swell**: dispatches up to 4 parallel eddy workers. - **swell_analyze**: parallel file analysis — eddies read directories and answer questions in parallel. - **swell_build**: parallel multi-component build — eddies write components in parallel. - **undertow**: QA. ALWAYS screenshot and verify HTML before delivering. - **break**: compile. shell_exec build after EVERY file_write. "Run the break." - **reef**: error / hazard. Fix directly with the right tool. Type/syntax errors → file_edit. Missing module → shell_exec npm install. Missing file → file_write. Wrong path (cd fails) → shell_exec with corrected path (NEVER message_chat — just fix it). CSS resolution errors → file_edit to remove or replace the import (the error tells you the line — don't file_read first). - **deep**: long-context state. Beyond shallow recent turns. - **tide**: time/iteration pressure. - **shore**: delivery. Land the wave at the shore. ## Before the Pipeline (pre-flight checks) - Visual clones (user says "looks like X" or "style of Y" or "clone of Z") → search_web FIRST for reference images/layout, THEN project_init - User explicitly asks for a plan ("plan needed", "plan carefully", "multi-step project") → plan_update FIRST - Default: go straight to project_init ## The Pipeline (ride the wave in this order) 1. project_init(name) — scaffold 2. file_write(App.tsx) — write COMPLETE code 3. shell_exec build — run the break 4. IF reef (error): fix directly — file_edit (type/syntax fix), file_write (missing file), or shell_exec (install module, corrected path) 5. undertow(dist/index.html) — QA before delivery 6. message_result — land the wave at shore ## Parallel pipeline (3+ components → swell) 1. project_init(name) 2. swell_build({components: [...]}) — fork eddies to write components in parallel 3. shell_exec build — run the break 4. undertow — QA 5. message_result — land the wave ## Components Import from "./components/ui" (NOT "../components/ui/button" or other subpaths). Available: Button, Card, Input, Badge, Dialog, Select, Progress, Avatar, Switch, Tooltip, Dropdown, Accordion, Alert, Skeleton. ## Rules - NEVER skip the break. - NEVER deliver without undertow. - One tool call per response. break } try { const delta = JSON.parse(data).choices?.[0]?.delta?.content if (delta) res.write(\`data: \${JSON.stringify({ delta })}\n\n\`) } catch {} } } } catch (e) { res.write(\`data: \${JSON.stringify({ delta: "[Error]" })}\n\n\`) res.write("data: [DONE]\n\n") } res.end() }) app.listen(3001, () => console.log("Chat proxy on :3001")) "}} model File written: workspace/deliverables/my-chatbot/server/index.js model {"name": "file_write", "parameters": {"path": "workspace/deliverables/my-chatbot/src/App.tsx", "content": "import { useState } from "react" import { useChat } from "./hooks/useChat" export default function App() { const [systemPrompt, setSystemPrompt] = useState("You are a helpful AI assistant.") const [editingSystem, setEditingSystem] = useState(false) const { messages, sendMessage, isStreaming, clearMessages } = useChat(systemPrompt) const [input, setInput] = useState("") function handleSend() { if (!input.trim() || isStreaming) return sendMessage(input.trim()) setInput("") } return (
